evidence.get_disk: Single disk only: Disk 0 SKHynix_HFS001TEM4X182N, 953.9 GB, NVMe, Online.
evidence.get_partition: Disk 0 partitions: System(0.3G), Reserved, C:(951.6G), Recovery(2G). No F: partition exists.
evidence.mountvol: Two unlettered volumes present are WINRE_DRV and SYSTEM_DRV recovery partitions, not a delettered F:.
evidence.powershell_get_psdrive_F: DriveNotFoundException: A drive with the name 'F' does not exist.
evidence.win32_logicaldisk: Only C: (951.6 GB, 75.96 GiB free). No F:.

blocker.summary: The relocation target F: is not present on the system. It is not merely unmounted or offline — there is only ONE physical disk installed. F: was an external/removable drive that has been physically disconnected since the earlier (2026-07-18/20) readiness snapshots that observed it with ~181-249 GiB free.
blocker.why_not_forced: Even if F: were re-attached, hosting a live 68 GiB Docker data VHDX on a removable drive that demonstrably disconnects is the direct cause of the crash-loops this task set out to fix. Retargeting Docker to a missing/removable F: would guarantee daemon failure and risk the CVAT volume data the task requires preserving. Correct autonomous action is a safety stop, not a forced move.

collateral_finding_and_fix.finding: The prior sibling relocation of the repo data/ directory to F:\MaskFactory_DataRelocated left a DANGLING junction once F: disconnected (dir C:\Comfy_UI_Main_Masking\data -> 'File Not Found'), breaking MASKFACTORY_DATA_PATH / CVAT read-only share.
collateral_finding_and_fix.fix: Repointed the data/ junction to the retained on-C: backup C:\Comfy_UI_Main_Masking\data_c_backup_relocated (2.98 GiB: cvat, cvat_v2, images, incoming, maskfactory.sqlite, packages). Non-destructive and reversible; no data deleted; can be repointed back to F: if the external drive returns.
collateral_finding_and_fix.idempotent: A parallel sibling had also repointed to the same C: backup target between probes; operation converged on the identical correct end state.

cvat_recovery.image_versions: cvat/server:v2.24.0, cvat/ui:v2.24.0 (production pinned stack, loopback localhost:8080)
cvat_recovery.post_state: All 17 cvat_* containers Up and stable; workers recovered and stayed up (no re-flap over verification window).
cvat_recovery.symptom_at_start: Production CVAT v2.24 flapping under disk pressure: cvat_server intermittently down (Bad Gateway) and cvat_worker_export/import/chunks/annotation Exited(255).

docker_state.note: Docker data remains on C: exactly where it was; nothing was moved, exported, imported, or retargeted.

final_snapshot.cvat_2_24_status: UP (localhost:8080; 17/17 containers up; SAM2 smoke pass)

kevin_actions_required: If Docker/data relocation off C: is still desired, provision a PERMANENT fixed second drive (not a removable/USB F:). Relocating live Docker data onto a removable drive is unsafe and reproduces the crash-loops.

If the external F: drive is intended to stay, reconnect it and confirm it is a fixed/always-present volume before any Docker or data/ retarget; the data/ junction can then be repointed from the C: backup back to F: on request.
C: free is 75.96 GiB — just above the 75 GiB ingest floor. Without a real second disk, further headroom must come from governed cleanup, not VHDX relocation.
requested_task: Relocate Docker Desktop data VHDX from C: to F: non-destructively (preserve CVAT volumes); quit Docker, wsl --shutdown, move/retarget, restart, verify CVAT 2.24.
